methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 20 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Kraken Robotics is a publicly listed company in the Defense & Security sector. The broader comp universe includes 2 public peers (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private companies, with top private peers including Anduril Industries, Saronic, Flock Safety, Shield AI, and Chaos Industries. This yields a total of 10 comparable companies across public and private markets.

Peer-median valuation analysis is not applicable in this case. The Defense & Security sector contains 20 same-sector peers overall, but filtering for comparable stage, maturity tier, and business model yields fewer than 3 datapoints across all narrow criteria (same-stage, adjacent-stage, and similar-valuation-tier combinations). A cross-sector median would introduce substantial methodological error and obscure sector-specific valuation drivers. Consequently, traditional multiples-based triangulation cannot be reliably executed.

Given these constraints, valuation of Kraken Robotics cannot be supported by standard peer multiples analysis. Alternative approaches—such as discounted cash flow modeling, transaction-based benchmarking from recent Defense & Security M&A, or revenue-run-rate analysis relative to the identified private peer set—would be required to establish a credible valuation range. The primary uncertainty remains the lack of tight comparables at similar scale and operational maturity within the sector.
